learning_outcomes.description: Explain the impact of aging and common age-related health conditions on communication and swallowing abilities in older adults. (SPA PS 1.1, 1.3, 1.6, 1.7, 3.1, 3.2).
Apply appropriate assessment tools and protocols to evaluate communication and swallowing disorders in adults across different clinical contexts (SPA PS 1.5, 3.2, 3.3).

Demonstrate understanding of evidence-based intervention approaches for managing communication and swallowing disorders across a range of populations, including stroke and neurodegenerative disease (SPA PS 1.1, 1.3, 3.4, 3.5).
Identify the principles of person and family-centred care when working with adults and their support networks in various healthcare settings (SPA PS 1.4, 1.6, 3.4).

Recognise the psychosocial impact of communication and swallowing disorders in adults and apply appropriate counselling strategies within the scope of speech pathology practice (SPA PS 1.4, 3.4, 3.5).
Discuss ethical considerations in providing speech pathology services within different populations and contexts, including in palliative care (SPA PS 1.1, 1.2, 1.6).

assessments.task_description:
There will be a series of five (5) weekly knowledge check quizzes in this course, which will be comprised of multiple choice questions. Your three (3) highest scoring quizzes will make up your 10% grade (i.e., each quiz will contribute equally, with your total percentage correct from the 3 highest quizzes being used to calculate the 10% weighting). The two (2) quizzes that you receive the lowest marks for (which may include any quizzes that are not submitted or are submitted late) will not contribute to your grade. Quizzes will cover content from weeks 2-6. Each quiz opens during the content week but is due the following Monday at 1pm (e.g., the Week 2 quiz opens in Week 2 and is due Monday of Week 3 by 1pm).

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All quiz answers must be submitted via an online link in blackboard. If a student wishes to change one or more of their answers after submission, they must resubmit their responses for ALL questions prior to the due date. Only the most recent submission attempt will be used to mark responses.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This examination will be an in-person on-campus School based In-semester examination. The exam will include both short answer and case-based questions and may cover all content included from weeks 1-6 of the semester. Further information will be provided during the semester to assist with preparation.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom O-week To Week 13(20 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Online self-directed learning

Pre-recorded lectures providing preparatory information will be released in O week for student viewing prior to commencement of the course. Specifically, these lectures will provide an overview of the course and associated assessment, as well as an introduction to aphasia, psycholinguistics and cognitive neuropsychology.

Pre-recorded lecture content will also be available on blackboard across weeks 2-13. It is expected that students will engage with this content prior to attending the weekly tutorials.

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

An on campus face to face 2-hour lecture will be provided in week 1 of semester introducing a psycholinguistic model of language processing.
learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures An on campus face to face 2-hour lecture will be provided in week 1 of semester introducing a psycholinguistic model of language processing.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(03 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Weekly on-campus Tutorials

Weekly 2-hour tutorials will be held in weeks 2-6 and 8-13 of semester. Tutorials will be practically-based and will facilitate student understanding and application of the course content.
